title: Documentation files: docs/knowledge-types.md (NEW) + docs/initialization.md (UPDATE) + docs/roadmap.md (UPDATE) + CHANGELOG.md (UPDATE)
scope: docs/knowledge-types.md (NEW); docs/initialization.md (UPDATE 466 lines → v2.0 flow); docs/roadmap.md (UPDATE 50 lines → v2.0 + v2.1 + v2.x); CHANGELOG.md (UPDATE 331 lines → add rc.1/rc.2/rc.3/rc.4 + v2.0.0 entries)
description: Author/update 4 documentation files comprising the v2.0 documentation surface. (a) docs/knowledge-types.md NEW: 5-type semantic definitions (decisions, pitfalls, guidelines, models, processes) with examples + decision criteria + maturity tier ref + layer classification ref. (b) docs/initialization.md UPDATE: replace v1.x init flow narrative with v2.0 flow (scan → install skills + hooks). (c) docs/roadmap.md UPDATE: add v2.0 (released) section + v2.1 (team-knowledge.git + 3-role permissions) + v2.x (semantic search, federated teams). (d) CHANGELOG.md UPDATE: add detailed entries for rc.1 (clean rebrand foundation) + rc.2 (archive loop) + rc.3 (review loop + path-traversal fix) + rc.4 (lint + import + README + docs) + v2.0.0 final release notes (stable signal + upgrade-from-v1.x guidance).

files.change: Sections: (1) Purpose paragraph — 'Fabric organizes knowledge into 5 types each with distinct purpose and lifecycle.'; (2) The 5 Types overview table (type | purpose | example | typical layer | typical maturity); (3) Per-type detailed sections (Decisions: ADRs and architectural choices; Pitfalls: known gotchas and anti-patterns; Guidelines: conventions and best practices; Models: domain models and core abstractions; Processes: how-to and operational runbooks); each with 2-3 concrete examples drawn from Fabric self-repo or generic dev examples; (4) Decision Criteria section — 'Which type should I use?' decision tree (3-5 questions narrowing to a type); (5) Maturity tiers reference — draft (newly proposed), endorsed (validated by team), stable (entrenched in practice); (6) Layer classification reference — team (project artifacts, .fabric/), personal ($FABRIC_HOME/.fabric/); (7) Cross-references to README.md + docs/initialization.md.

files.target: full file rewrite (466 lines v1.x → ~300-400 lines v2.0)
files.change: Replace v1.x narrative with v2.0 flow: (1) Overview — `fabric init` does 3 things: scan, install skills, install hooks; (2) Pre-flight checks (project root detection, package manager detection, existing .fabric/ check); (3) Init-time scan (Phase 1 of fabric-import) — produces 4-7 baseline entries from tech stack, module structure, build config, code style, CI, README first paragraph; lands in .fabric/knowledge/team/; (4) Skill installation — 3 skills (fabric-archive, fabric-review, fabric-import) copied to .claude/skills/ + .codex/skills/; (5) Pointer installation — one-line skill references added to CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules; (6) Hook installation — archive-hint.cjs Stop hook copied + 3 client config files (claude-code.json / cursor-hooks.json / codex-hooks.toml); (7) Post-init verification — `fabric doctor` should report 0 fixable findings; (8) Cross-references to docs/knowledge-types.md + README.md + docs/roadmap.md.

files.target: full file rewrite (50 lines pre-v2 → ~150-200 lines)
files.change: Sections: (1) v2.0 (Released — 2026-Q2): summary of what shipped per RC (rc.1 clean rebrand + init scan; rc.2 archive loop + extract knowledge + Stop hooks; rc.3 review loop + skill mode inference + filesystem-edit fallback; rc.4 lint + import skill + README + docs); link to CHANGELOG.md for detail; (2) v2.1 (Planned): team-knowledge.git remote sync (centralized team knowledge in a git repo with pull/push primitives); 3-role permission model (admin / contributor / reader); event-ledger schema unlock (knowledge_layer_change_started + agents_meta_repaired); pending_path → target_path API rename; (3) v2.x (Exploration): semantic search (embeddings + vector index in .fabric/.embeddings/); federated teams (multiple teams sharing subsets via signed knowledge bundles); doctor --apply-lint configurability of decay thresholds; (4) Out-of-Scope explicit list: no SaaS hosting in roadmap; no LLM hosting in roadmap; no UI dashboard rebuild (existing dashboard stays MVP).

files.target: extend existing 331 lines with detailed rc.1/rc.2/rc.3/rc.4 + v2.0.0 entries
files.change: Add new sections at top (descending chronological): (1) `## [v2.0.0] — 2026-05-11` Final v2.0 stable release; what's new since v1.x (knowledge cycle: archive → review → promote → lint → archive; 4 MCP tools; 3 Skills; Stop hooks across 3 clients; dual-root layout; lifecycle/lint moat); upgrade guide v1.x → v2.0 (delete .fabric/rules/, run `fabric init`); breaking changes (.fabric tree restructured, stable_id format changed, schemas updated); (2) `## [v2.0.0-rc.4] — 2026-05-11` rc.4 milestone; doctor --lint with 6 deterministic checks; doctor --apply-lint mutations + events; fabric-import skill template; README rewrite; docs/{knowledge-types,initialization,roadmap}.md authored/updated; rc.3 deferred fixes batch; (3) `## [v2.0.0-rc.3] — 2026-05-10` rc.3 milestone; fab_review MCP tool (6 actions); fabric-review skill mode inference; archive-hint second signal pending overflow; doctor filesystem-edit fallback; path-traversal sandboxing fix (Critical); per-file >=90% coverage gate; (4) `## [v2.0.0-rc.2] — 2026-05-10` rc.2 milestone; fab_extract_knowledge MCP tool; fabric-archive skill template; archive-hint Stop hook + 3 client config files; (5) `## [v2.0.0-rc.1] — 2026-05-XX` rc.1 milestone; clean rebrand foundation; .fabric/rules deleted; .fabric/knowledge structure created; init-time scan; new schemas (api-contracts.ts, agents-meta.ts, event-ledger.ts); KnowledgeIdAllocator. Preserve all existing pre-v2 entries below.

implementation: Read current docs/initialization.md (466 lines) and docs/roadmap.md (50 lines) to identify content to preserve, replace, or rewrite
Read current CHANGELOG.md (331 lines) to identify last entry header and confirm chronological pattern (Keep-a-Changelog or custom format)

Author docs/knowledge-types.md from scratch using planning-context.md + handoff.json + key_findings as source material; concrete examples drawn from .fabric/knowledge/team/ if entries exist (likely 3 rc.2 + rc.3 dogfood entries available; cite them as live examples)

Rewrite docs/initialization.md: keep any pre-flight detection logic that's still accurate; replace `fabric init` flow description with v2.0 (scan + install skills + install hooks); cross-link to knowledge-types.md and roadmap.md

Rewrite docs/roadmap.md: explicit v2.0 (Released) / v2.1 (Planned) / v2.x (Exploration) tiered structure; out-of-scope explicit; mention .workflow/.lite-plan sessions as evidence of execution discipline

Update CHANGELOG.md: add 5 new section blocks at top in reverse-chronological order; preserve format (likely Keep-a-Changelog with Added/Changed/Deprecated/Removed/Fixed/Security subsections per release); for each RC, list 5-8 bulleted highlights; for v2.0.0 final release, include upgrade guide
Verify consistency: README.md links (TASK-007) point to these files; section anchors match; no broken cross-references
Verify no contradictions vs README, planning-context.md, handoff.json (decay thresholds, schema versions, etc.)
Run markdown lint on all 4 files (markdownlint or equivalent) — 0 errors
Verify total docs/ tree length post-update fits 'comprehensive but not bloated' (knowledge-types ~200 lines, initialization ~350 lines, roadmap ~150 lines, CHANGELOG +200 lines)
Commit as 'docs: knowledge-types + initialization + roadmap + CHANGELOG for v2.0 (rc.4 TASK-008)'

reference.pattern: Existing docs/* files for style; Keep-a-Changelog format for CHANGELOG; planning-context.md for content sourcing
reference.examples: Karpathy gist for terse style; existing CHANGELOG.md format; rc.2/rc.3 plan.json + dogfood-evidence.md for milestone bullets

criteria: docs/knowledge-types.md exists with all 5 types defined: decisions, pitfalls, guidelines, models, processes
Each of the 5 types has at least 2 concrete examples in docs/knowledge-types.md
docs/knowledge-types.md has decision-criteria section with 3-5 questions
docs/knowledge-types.md references maturity tiers (draft/endorsed/stable) and layer classification (team/personal)
docs/initialization.md describes v2.0 flow (scan → install skills + hooks) — verified by grep for 'fabric init' steps narrative
docs/initialization.md does NOT reference deleted .fabric/rules/ or v1.x INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md — verified by absence of these strings
docs/roadmap.md has explicit v2.0 (Released) section with 4 RC milestone summaries
docs/roadmap.md has v2.1 (Planned) section listing team-knowledge.git + 3-role permissions + schema unlocks
docs/roadmap.md has v2.x (Exploration) section listing semantic search + federated teams
CHANGELOG.md has 5 new section blocks (rc.1, rc.2, rc.3, rc.4, v2.0.0) in reverse-chronological order at top
CHANGELOG.md v2.0.0 entry has upgrade-from-v1.x guide
All 4 files pass markdown lint with 0 errors
Cross-references verified: README links → docs/* files; docs/* internal links resolve

rationale.chosen_approach: Single-task batch covering 4 doc files; rationale: docs are interconnected (cross-references between them), atomic landing keeps them consistent; alternative of separate tasks per doc creates merge churn for cross-link updates
alternatives_considered: Split into 4 separate tasks (one per doc) — rejected: cross-references would require merge serialization; atomic batch keeps docs consistent

Skip CHANGELOG.md update in TASK-008, defer to TASK-010 — rejected: TASK-010 final entry needs CHANGELOG already containing rc.1-rc.4 entries to add v2.0.0 release notes; landing CHANGELOG bulk in TASK-008 leaves only the v2.0.0 final entry for TASK-010
Auto-generate roadmap.md from issue tracker — rejected: no issue tracker integration; manual roadmap is fine for v2.0
Skip docs/knowledge-types.md, embed in skill prose only — rejected: handoff explicitly requires NEW file; users authoring entries need a reference outside skill context
decision_factors: CHANGELOG bulk landing in TASK-008 leaves v2.0.0 final entry as TASK-10's clean concluding act
knowledge-types.md is reference doc consumed by skills + users
rationale.tradeoffs: Single large commit; mitigated by 4 logically-distinct sections in one PR; reviewer can read each section independently

manual_checks: Render all 4 files in a markdown viewer; verify sections + tables + cross-links
Verify README.md TASK-007 links resolve (open each link, confirm target exists)
Read knowledge-types.md as a new user authoring a first entry; verify decision criteria are actionable
Read initialization.md as a new user running fabric init; verify steps match actual init.ts behavior

risks.description: knowledge-types.md examples may drift from real usage as users author entries
risks.mitigation: Use generic dev examples (login service decision, N+1 query pitfall, snake-case naming guideline) that age slowly; supplement with Fabric self-repo entries cited by stable_id (will need re-citation if those entries are demoted/archived)

risks.description: CHANGELOG entries may differ in style/depth across the 5 sections, looking inconsistent
risks.mitigation: Use uniform Keep-a-Changelog subsection structure (Added/Changed/Fixed) per section; extract bullets from rc.2/rc.3 plan.json + dogfood-evidence.md for consistent depth; v2.0.0 final entry follows same structure
risks.fallback: Style cleanup pass in dogfood TASK-009

risks.description: Roadmap v2.1 + v2.x bullets may overcommit to features we don't ship
risks.mitigation: Frame v2.1 as 'Planned' (intent + design discussion) and v2.x as 'Exploration' (open question); use language like 'we are exploring' / 'under consideration'; explicit Out-of-Scope list disclaims SaaS / LLM hosting / UI rebuild
risks.fallback: Roadmap is updated each release; correct course in v2.0.x patches if needed

key_functions.purpose: 5-type semantic ref + examples + decision criteria + maturity + layer cross-ref
v2.0 init flow: scan → install skills → install hooks
rc.1 / rc.2 / rc.3 / rc.4 / v2.0.0 entries (Keep-a-Changelog format)
